text: Science has a clearer story about homologous recombination than social media does. Homologous recombination is a natural process where DNA strands exchange genetic material. This mechanism is crucial for repairing DNA, ensuring genetic diversity. Scientists have confirmed that homologous recombination occurs during meiosis, the process that creates gametes. This ensures offspring inherit a mix of traits, making life more adaptable. Curious about more science mysteries? Follow for insights!
